verbatim_quote: "The north remembers, Lord Davos. The north remembers, and the mummer's farce is almost done. My son is home." Something about the way Lord Wyman said that chilled Davos to the bone.
note: Of the two ambiguous hits, Davos IV:125 (Manderly's monologue about vengeance) is the better match for the paraphrase about Northern lords secretly plotting against the Boltons, as it explicitly details Manderly's conspiracy against the Freys and reflects the theme of secret resentment.

verbatim_quote: With Rowan's help, Theon got Jeyne Poole into Squirrel's clothes. If the gods are good and the guards are blind, she may pass. "Now we are going out and down the steps," Theon told the girl. "Keep your head down and your hood up. Follow Holly. Don't run, don't cry, don't speak, don't look anyone in the eye."
note: Explicitly shows Jeyne Poole being dressed in serving-girl disguise by the wildling women (Rowan, Holly, Squirrel, etc.) to escape Winterfell and Ramsay's wedding.

verbatim_quote: The dead man was found at the base of the inner wall, with his neck broken and only his left leg showing above the snow that had buried him during the night.
note: First of the Winterfell murders: Ryswell groom found with broken neck at base of inner wall.

verbatim_quote: The next morning Ser Aenys Frey's grizzled squire was found naked and dead of exposure in the old castle lichyard, his face so obscured by hoarfrost that he appeared to be wearing a mask.
note: Second murder: Frey squire found naked in the castle graveyard/lichyard.

verbatim_quote: Then, before the day was done, a crossbowman sworn to the Flints turned up in the stables with a broken skull.
note: Third death: Flint soldier found in stables with broken skull.

verbatim_quote: The dead man was one of Ramsay's favorites, the squat, scrofulous, ill-favored man-at-arms called Yellow Dick. Whether his dick had actually been yellow was hard to determine, as someone had sliced it off and stuffed it into his mouth so forcefully they had broken three of his teeth.
note: Of the two ambiguous hits, Ghost in Winterfell:101 is the precise match for the specific mutilation described (severed member forced into mouth, teeth broken).

verbatim_quote: "Under that ruined keep, my lord," replied Big Walder. "The one with the old gargoyles." The boy's gloves were caked with his cousin's blood. "I told him not to go out alone, but he said he had to find a man who owed him silver."
note: Little Walder Frey found dead, with Big Walder's gloves caked in blood; presented as fifth victim escalating tensions.

verbatim_quote: Farther on, he came upon a man striding in the opposite direction, a hooded cloak flapping behind him. When they found themselves face-to-face their eyes met briefly. The man put a hand on his dagger. "Theon Turncloak. Theon Kinslayer."
note: Theon's direct encounter with the Hooded Man in the snow at night; the man wears a hooded cloak and confronts Theon alone.

verbatim_quote: "Theon Turncloak. Theon Kinslayer."
note: Hooded Man directly calls Theon 'Turncloak' in their face-to-face encounter.

verbatim_quote: "Theon Turncloak. Theon Kinslayer."
note: Hooded Man directly calls Theon 'Kinslayer' in same line.

verbatim_quote: The man put a hand on his dagger.
note: Hooded Man puts hand on dagger during the encounter; combined with next line shows he then laughs and leaves.

verbatim_quote: "The gods are not done with me," Theon answered, wondering if this could be the killer, the night walker who had stuffed Yellow Dick's cock into his mouth and pushed Roger Ryswell's groom off the battlements.
note: Theon explicitly suspects the Hooded Man might be the Winterfell murderer based on the man's prowling at night with a dagger.

verbatim_quote: "Not us." Rowan grabbed him by the throat and shoved him back against the barracks wall, her face an inch from his. "Say it again and I will rip your lying tongue out, kinslayer."
note: Rowan calls Theon 'kinslayer'; combined with other beats establishes this as characteristic language used by those who hate Theon for supposedly killing Stark boys.

verbatim_quote: Rowan gave him a hard look. "You have no right to mouth Lord Eddard's words. Not you. Not ever. After what you did—"
note: Rowan's hatred for Theon is explicit; she spits in his face (line 123) and holds him responsible for Stark children's deaths.

verbatim_quote: Rowan gave him a hard look. "You have no right to mouth Lord Eddard's words. Not you. Not ever. After what you did—"
note: Already matched in input; Rowan's respect for Eddard Stark despite being wildling shows deep connection to Northern loyalism.

verbatim_quote: The Night's Watch takes no part, Jon thought, but another voice within him said, Words are not swords. "The elder of the Greatjon's uncles. Crowfood, they call him. A crow once took him for dead and pecked out his eye. He caught the bird in his fist and bit its head off. When Mors was young he was a fearsome fighter. His sons died on the Trident, his wife in childbed. His only daughter was carried off by wildlings thirty years ago."
note: Already matched in input; establishes Crowfood Umber had a daughter taken by wildlings ~30 years prior, raising possibility Rowan is that daughter.

verbatim_quote: When they found themselves face-to-face their eyes met briefly.
note: Of two ambiguous hits for 'face-to-face', Ghost in Winterfell:127 is the precise match describing the Hooded Man encounter where they meet face-to-face in the snow; the hood doesn't hide his face.

verbatim_quote: But there was no use telling Rowan any of that. She would never believe his denials, any more than he believed hers. "There is blood on my hands, but not the blood of brothers," he said wearily. "And I've been punished."
note: Theon is thinner and changed since Rowan might have known him; earlier narrative establishes his dramatic physical decline under torture supports the theory he might not be recognized.

verbatim_quote: "My lord," boomed Hosteen Frey. "We know the man who did this. Killed this boy and all the rest. Not by his own hand, no. He is too fat and craven to do his own killing. But by his word." He turned to Wyman Manderly. "Do you deny it?"
note: Hosteen Frey directly accuses Lord Wyman Manderly of orchestrating all the murders, leading to the Frey-Manderly conflict that erupts into violence.

verbatim_quote: "I see you all want blood," the Lord of the Dreadfort said. Maester Rhodry stood beside him, a raven on his arm. The bird's black plumage shone like coal oil in the torchlight. Wet, Theon realized. And in his lordship's hand, a parchment. That will be wet as well. Dark wings, dark words. "Rather than use our swords upon each other, you might try them on Lord Stannis." Lord Bolton unrolled the parchment. "His host lies not three days' ride from here, snowbound and starving, and I for one am tired of waiting on his pleasure. Ser Hosteen, assemble your knights and men-at-arms by the main gates. As you are so eager for battle, you shall strike our first blow. Lord Wyman, gather your White Harbor men by the east gate. They shall go forth as well."
note: Roose Bolton explicitly orders both the Freys and Manderlys to march out and attack Stannis, seemingly to stop their internal conflict.

verbatim_quote: "And Little Walder was a piglet. Killing him brought the Freys and Manderlys to dagger points, that was cunning, you—"
note: Theon notes that Little Walder's death specifically escalated tensions between the Freys and Manderlys, suggesting a targeted pattern to the victims.

verbatim_quote: Roger Ryswell grunted. "If not him, who? Stannis has some man inside the castle, that's plain."
note: The lords explicitly discuss their suspicion that Stannis has planted a man/agent inside Winterfell to commit the murders.

verbatim_quote: Theon grabbed Jeyne about the waist and jumped.
note: Theon and Jeyne jump from the outer wall at this exact moment; narrative indicates Crowfood is waiting below per the theory's implications.

No direct textual passage states Rowan is Crowfood's daughter; theory is speculative inference from Crowfood's mention of his lost daughter + Rowan's deep loyalty to the Starks + her wildling background.
The text does not provide sequential timing data showing Hooded Man and Crowfood arriving on the same night; this is a theoretical inference.

The Hooded Man calls Theon 'Turncloak' and 'Kinslayer' in one encounter (Ghost in Winterfell:127); Crowfood's epithets for Theon appear in Jon IV:213 context but not directly quoted together in comparative moment.

verbatim_quote: Squirrel slipped away, soft-footed as she always was. Rowan walked Theon from the hall. Since she and her sisters had found him in the godswood, one of them had dogged his every step, never letting him out of sight. They did not trust him. Why should they? I was Reek before and might be Reek again.
note: Explicitly establishes Rowan and wildling women were sent by someone (the text implies Melisandre/Stannis per Jon chapters) to infiltrate Winterfell.

verbatim_quote: Robett Glover broke in to add, "Your loyalty does you honor, my lord, but Stannis Baratheon remains your king, not our own."
note: Robett Glover appears at White Harbor with Wyman Manderly, confirming his presence in the anti-Bolton conspiracy sphere.

verbatim_quote: "Soon I must return to the feast to toast my friends of Frey," Manderly continued. "They watch me, ser. Day and night their eyes are on me, noses sniffing for some whiff of treachery. You saw them, the arrogant Ser Jared and his nephew Rhaegar, that smirking worm who wears a dragon's name. Behind them both stands Symond, clinking coins. That one has bought and paid for several of my servants and two of my knights. One of his wife's handmaids has found her way into the bed of my own fool. If Stannis wonders that my letters say so little, it is because I dare not even trust my maester."
note: Manderly's monologue establishes his secretive coordination with Stannis while maintaining the facade of loyalty to the Freys, consistent with larger conspiracy.
Text does not explicitly state what specific items Robett carries or wears during his plotting with Manderly; inference from cloak/dagger use is speculative.

verbatim_quote: "I begged my husband not to go. He had kin he might have sent in his stead. An uncle famed for his prowess with an axe, a great-uncle who had fought in the War of the Ninepenny Kings. But he was a man and full of pride, nothing would serve but that he lead the Barrowton levies himself. I gave him a horse the day he set out, a red stallion with a fiery mane, the pride of my lord father's herds. My lord swore that he would ride him home when the war was done."
note: Lady Dustin's narrative (via Theon chapter) establishes how much House Ryswell/Dustin lost, providing motive for Robett Glover to oppose Bolton loyalists.

verbatim_quote: The north remembers, Lord Davos. The north remembers, and the mummer's farce is almost done. My son is home." Something about the way Lord Wyman said that chilled Davos to the bone. "If it is justice that you want, my lord, look to King Stannis. No man is more just." Robett Glover broke in to add, "Your loyalty does you honor, my lord, but Stannis Baratheon remains your king, not our own."
note: Robett is present and aware of Manderly's anti-Bolton conspiracy with Stannis, so he would know Bran and Rickon are alive per the conspiracy knowledge-base.
No text directly states alternative kinslayer theories (Moat Cailin ironborn deaths, or Theon's supposed sons). These are fan theories not grounded in explicit book passages.

verbatim_quote: "Take the boys," Ned said to Harwin as the morning bells began to ring in the Red Keep, signaling the change of watch.
note: Harwin established as Ned's trusted household guard and teacher to Stark children in early books.

verbatim_quote: Behind him, Arya recognised the brothers' sworn swords. Harwin had gone with them, she remembered. Harwin's broad face as he fell into the river...
note: Establishes Harwin was sent with Beric Dondarrion and the group that became the Brotherhood, and his later reunification with Arya.

verbatim_quote: "Did the man give a name?" asked Sam. "Harwin. A northman, the brothers said, one of the old Stark men-at-arms. He was searching for his daughter. She was missing, he believed, carried off by a brigand."
note: Harwin appears with Lady Stoneheart's Brotherhood group searching for Arya (whom they believe is Lord Eddard's missing daughter).
While Harwin is in the Brotherhood sphere that would rescue fake-Arya, no text explicitly states his assignment to or successful infiltration of Winterfell to kill Bolton men.

verbatim_quote: In songs, the hero always saved the maiden from the monster's castle, but life was not a song, no more than Jeyne was Arya Stark. Her eyes are the wrong color. And there are no heroes here, only whores.
note: Theon's internal reflection on Harwin-like loyalty: Harwin knew Theon as a boy and would view Bran/Rickon's supposed deaths as kinslaying, creating motive consistent with Harwin as rescuer.
